data: Small rectangular plaque, with irregular upper and lower margins, broken on the right and left sides
Found in Lipari, but the exact place, circumstances and date of discovery are unknown. Taken to the Archaeological Museum of Palermo
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J. Prag 2023-07-28
Museo Archeologico Regionale Eoliano "Luigi Bernab� Brea"

The enormous collection is displayed in several neighbouring buildings, each with a particular focus: Prehistory, Prehistory of the minor islands, Epigraphy, Geology, the Classical period and later. The finds come from across the Aeolian islands, under the sea that surrounds them, and from the site of Milazzo on Sicily. One of the 3 largest Sicilian collections, it is truly comprehensive in scope and contains a few finds (e.g. the terracotta tragic theatre masks) that are near unique
Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus

G. Kaibel, Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus, Inscriptiones Graecae consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae Editae. Volumen XIV., XIV (Berlin: Georgius Reimerus, 1890)
Meligunìs Lipára. XII. Le iscrizioni lapidarie greche e latine delle isole eolie
L. Bernabò Brea, M. Cavalier, e L. Campagna, Meligunìs Lipára. XII. Le iscrizioni lapidarie greche e latine delle isole eolie (Palermo: Mario Grispo Editore, 2003)
Le isole eolie nell'antichità greca e romana. Ricerche storiche ed archeologiche
Guido Libertini, Le isole eolie nell’antichità greca e romana. Ricerche storiche ed archeologiche (Florence, 1921)

Text encoding: Andoni Llamazares
1Θεοῖς (vac.3) καταχθονίοις
2Καππαδόκων ἔθνους πολυανθέας οἴδατε ἀρούρας
3κεῖθεν ἐγὼ φυόμην ἐκ τοκέων ἀγαθῶν
4ἐξέτι τοὺς λιπόμην δύσιν ἤλυθον ἠδὲ καὶ ἠῶ
5οὔνομά μοι Γλάφυρος καὶ φρενὸς εἴκελον ἦ(ν)
6ἑξηκοστὸν´ ἔτος πανελεύθερον ἐξεβίωσα
7καὶ καλὸν τὸ τύχης καὶ πικρὸν οἶδα βίου

Text encoding: Dmitry Dundua, Robert Crellin
Θεοῖς (vac.3) καταχθονίοις Καππαδόκων ἔθνους πολυανθέας οἴδατε ἀρούρας κεῖθεν ἐγὼ φυόμην ἐκ τοκέων ἀγαθῶν ἐξέτι τοὺς λιπόμην δύσιν ἤλυθον ἠδὲ καὶ ἠῶ οὔνομά μοι Γλάφυρος καὶ φρενὸς εἴκελον ἦν ἑξηκοστὸν ἔτος πανελεύθερον ἐξεβίωσα καὶ καλὸν τὸ τύχης καὶ πικρὸν οἶδα βίου

7ΚΑΙΚΑΛΟΝΤΟΤΥΧΗΣΚΑΙΠΙΚΡΟΝΟΙΔΑΒΙΟΥ

Text encoding: Dmitry Dundua, Robert Crellin
ΘΕΟΙΣ        ΚΑΤΑΧΘΟΝΙΟΙΣΚΑΠΠΑΔΟΚΩΝΕΘΝΟΥΣΠΟΛΥΑΝΘΕΑΣΟΙΔΑΤΕΑΡΟΥΡΑΣΚΕΙΘΕΝΕΓΩΦΥΟΜΗΝΕΚΤΟΚΕΩΝΑΓΑΘΩΝΕΞΕΤΙΤΟΥΣΛΙΠΟΜΗΝΔΥΣΙΝΗΛΥΘΟΝΗΔΕΚΑΙΗΩΟΥΝΟΜΑΜΟΙΓΛΑΦΥΡΟΣΚΑΙΦΡΕΝΟΣΕΙΚΕΛΟΝΗΝΕΞΗΚΟΣΤΟΝΕΤΟΣΠΑΝΕΛΕΥΘΕΡΟΝΕΞΕΒΙΩΣΑΚΑΙΚΑΛΟΝΤΟΤΥΧΗΣΚΑΙΠΙΚΡΟΝΟΙΔΑΒΙΟΥ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • text according to I.Lipara ;
  • 2: οἶδα τεLibertini
  • 3: φύομενLibertini
  • 4: ἐξότεRitschl ; ἐξ[ό]τ [ε] Kaibel (1878)
  • 6: ΕΚΗΚΟΣΤΟΝCamarda ; πανελεύθεροςCamarda, Ritschl

English translation

Andoni Llamazares

To the Underworld Gods. You know about the flowerful fields of the Cappadocian people. There I was born from good parents. After I left them, I went to the West and to the East. My name is Glaphyr (elegant) and so is my soul. I lived until my sixtieth year being completely free, and knew the beauty of fortune and the bitterness of life.

commentary

bibliography

  • Libertini 1921 219 no.18 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001875
  • Bernabó-Brea Cavalier Campagna 2003 376 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000541

Phi: 22-24mm
